user_query:
I’m setting up a simple event-triggered ingest for new space footage metadata. Please build a local automation that treats the presence of input/incoming/new_shots.jsonl as the event and processes only items whose id is not already in input/catalog/master_catalog.json.

Inputs to use:
- input/catalog/master_catalog.json (existing catalog of processed shots)
- input/incoming/new_shots.jsonl (new shot metadata; may include duplicates)
- input/draft_update.md (a verbose draft note with placeholders)

Produce these outputs:
1) output/ingest/new_shots_catalog.json
   - JSON array of newly ingested shots only (exclude any whose id already exists in master_catalog).
   - Fields per item: id, title, capture_date (YYYY-MM-DD), duration_sec (int), duration_min (duration_sec/60 rounded to 1 decimal), tags (array sorted ascending), camera, location, notes (trimmed of leading/trailing whitespace).
   - Sort items by capture_date ascending.

2) output/ingest/new_shots_catalog.csv
   - Same items and order as the JSON above.
   - Columns: id,title,capture_date,duration_sec,duration_min,tags,camera,location (omit notes in CSV). Represent tags as a single string joined by ‘|’ in the same sorted order.

3) output/reports/ingest_status.md
   - Compose a short status report for the team that includes:
     • New shots ingested: an integer count.
     • Skipped (duplicates): how many from new_shots.jsonl had ids already present in master_catalog.
     • Total duration of newly ingested shots in minutes (sum of duration_sec/60, rounded to 1 decimal).
     • Capture date range across new shots (earliest → latest).
     • Tag frequency counts across new shots (each tag with its count), sorted by count desc then tag asc.
     • A details list of each newly ingested shot with id, title, capture_date, and duration_min.

4) output/communications/revised_update.md
   - Rewrite input/draft_update.md to be concise and inspiring in tone (confident, visionary), 60–120 words.
   - Replace the placeholders {NEW_COUNT}, {TOTAL_MINUTES}, and {ID_LIST} with computed values from this ingest:
       · {NEW_COUNT} = number of newly ingested shots.
       · {TOTAL_MINUTES} = total minutes rounded to 1 decimal, matching the report.
       · {ID_LIST} = comma-separated list of new shot ids in capture_date order.
   - Preserve the factual content (counts and IDs) while removing hedging and redundancy.

5) output/catalog/updated_master_catalog.json
   - Merge master_catalog.json with the newly ingested shots (unique by id), sorted by capture_date ascending.

Notes:
- Treat the event as “new metadata file present at input/incoming/new_shots.jsonl”; do not alter input files.
- All outputs should be fully determined by the inputs; no network access is needed.
- Make all three basic operations essential: write structured results (JSON/CSV), compose the status report, and rewrite the draft message for tone/clarity/brevity while filling the placeholders.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
